applied_optimizations.name: Correct Triton kernel implementation
applied_optimizations.description: Fixed weight tensor indexing (ConvTranspose1d weight shape is (in_channels, out_channels, kernel_size)). Removed continue statements (not supported in Triton) and used boolean masking instead.

applied_optimizations.name: Optimized block sizes
applied_optimizations.description: Increased BLOCK_OUT_CHANNEL from 8 to 64 and BLOCK_OUTPUT_LENGTH from 64 to 512 to reduce grid dimensions and improve GPU utilization. Progress: 9.9ms → 4.43ms → 3.55ms → 2.64ms

attempted_but_failed.name: tl.dot matrix multiplication
attempted_but_failed.reason: LLVM translation failures on ROCm Triton
attempted_but_failed.error: failed to translate module to LLVM IR - builtin.unrealized_conversion_cast

attempted_but_failed.name: Atomic add approach
attempted_but_failed.reason: Extreme performance regression due to serialization

attempted_but_failed.name: One block per output channel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Too many blocks, insufficient work per block

attempted_but_failed.name: 1D tl.sum reduction per output channel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Too many thread blocks (262K), high launch overhead

top_bottlenecks: Loop iterations (96 per thread block: 32 in_channels × 3 kernel_size)
Scalar FMAs instead of matrix multiply units (tl.dot unavailable on ROCm)
Sparse memory access pattern due to stride=2

notes: tl.dot consistently fails with LLVM translation errors on ROCm - fundamental limitation
Key optimization: increasing block sizes to reduce grid dimensions
Remaining gap due to 96 loop iterations and inability to use MFMA units
To beat PyTorch would require working tl.dot or fundamentally different algorithm

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
